dependencies: Hotel owners for property development and operations
Third-party franchisees and licensees for brand operations
Co-branded credit card partners (JPMorgan Chase, American Express) for Loyalty Program funding
Technology service providers for reservation and property management systems
Centralized programs and services providers (marketing, reservations, IT infrastructure)
risks: Dependence on hotel owners' ability to obtain financing for property development and renovations
Reliance on third-party hotel owners and franchisees for brand standards compliance
Concentration of incentive management fees from International managed hotels (67% in 2024)
Dependence on co-branded credit card agreements for Loyalty Program funding
Reliance on third-party service providers for technology systems and cybersecurity
Supply chain disruptions from natural disasters and extreme weather events

regulatory_risks: Employment practices regulations (labor laws, collective bargaining, wage requirements)
Marketing and advertising regulations (consumer protection, data privacy in marketing)
Consumer protection laws (pricing transparency, guest rights)
Trade and economic sanctions (U.S. sanctions on countries like Iran, Russia, Cuba)
Anti-bribery, anti-corruption, and anti-money laundering laws (FCPA, UK Bribery Act)
Intellectual property regulations (trademark protection, licensing requirements)
Cybersecurity and data privacy laws (GDPR in EU, CCPA in California, data localization requirements)
Data transfer restrictions (cross-border data flow regulations)
Climate and environmental regulations (emissions reduction targets, sustainability reporting)
Health, safety, and accessibility regulations (building codes, ADA compliance, food safety)
Liquor sales regulations (licensing requirements by jurisdiction)
Franchise disclosure and registration laws (FTC Franchise Rule, state franchise laws)
Credit card product regulations (consumer financial protection laws)
Data Security Incident regulatory resolutions (FTC and 49 state AG settlements with ongoing compliance requirements)
New or changing tax laws and interpretations (transfer pricing, digital services taxes)
Labor regulation changes (unionization requirements, work rules)

sanction_risks: U.S. trade and economic sanctions compliance (Iran, Russia, Cuba, other sanctioned countries)
Risk of operations being impacted by sanctions on countries where hotels operate
Potential for increased sanctions affecting hotel operations and development
Compliance costs and operational constraints from evolving sanctions regimes
